title: alpinia coriacea
intro: Alpinia coriacea is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae), commonly known as a type of ginger. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Alpinia coriacea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of colds and flu.
